commits.message: removed "about" section, fixed the display year and tax boundaries to change dynamically
fixed local testing issue, amended SEO/meta block for search engines
The code I added will automatically redirect users from HTTP to HTTPS when they're not on localhost, ensuring secure connections in production.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the NetToGros_Qoder project. Explain its purpose as a Progressive Web App (PWA) designed for calculating Irish payroll taxes, including net-to-gross and gross-to-net salary conversions. Describe the core architecture as a monolithic single-page application with embedded JavaScript, CSS, and HTML in index.html, supported by manifest.json for PWA metadata and sw.js for offline functionality via service worker. Highlight the relationship between the three files and their roles in enabling a reliable, installable web experience. Document the lack of external dependencies and reliance on native browser APIs. Include a high-level explanation of how the app supports different tax statuses (single, married, single parent), time periods, and year-specific tax rates. Provide context for beginners on what a PWA is and why this app benefits from that model. For developers, summarize the procedural JavaScript structure, calculation flow, and caching strategy. Include a simple system diagram showing user interaction, calculation pipeline, and service worker caching layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for end users explaining how to use the NetToGros_Qoder payroll calculator. Start with a step-by-step guide for both calculation types: entering salary amounts, selecting tax status, choosing time period, and interpreting results. Clarify the difference between gross and net income in the context of Irish taxation. Explain each tax component (PAYE, USC, PRSI) in plain language with real-world examples. Provide instructions for switching between years to view historical tax calculations. Include guidance on understanding the breakdown table output, including deductions and take-home pay. Add a dedicated section on installing the PWA on mobile and desktop devices, leveraging the manifest.json and service worker functionality. Explain the benefits of installation, such as offline access and home screen launching. Use annotated screenshots (if available) or descriptive scenarios to enhance clarity. Address common user mistakes like entering commas in numbers or misunderstanding tax credits.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed technical documentation for the implementation of the calculator's logic and PWA capabilities. For the calculation engine in index.html, document the structure of tax rate tables, credit values, and bracket configurations used for PAYE, USC, and PRSI. Explain the algorithmic approach in calculateNetFromGross() and calculateGrossFromNet(), particularly how iterative approximation is used for reverse calculation. Break down the helper functions for tax band processing and percentage application. For sw.js, detail the cache-first strategy, including which assets are cached (self) and how fetch events are intercepted. Explain the service worker lifecycle registration in index.html. Discuss limitations of the current monolithic design and potential improvements such as modularization or external configuration. Include performance considerations like fast startup due to caching and minimal bundle size. Provide code examples showing event listeners, cache storage usage, and error handling in the service worker.
